fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Missouri Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Missouri - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity missouri, how to transfer dietitian license to missouri, dietitian compact missouri, missouri dietitian endorsement application

board.name: State Committee of Dietitians
board.mailing_address:
Missouri Board of Healing Arts
3605 Missouri Boulevard
P.O. Box 4
Jefferson City, MO 65102

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (by April 1, even years) — no CE required

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice dietetics in Missouri. Applicants must hold current CDR registration (RD/RDN), be at least 21 years old, and be a U.S. citizen or authorized to work in Missouri.

steps.title: Create MoRLD Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Access the Missouri Board of Healing Arts initial application page and click the 'Apply Online' link to the MoRLD (Missouri Real-time Licensing Database) system. If you're a new user, create an account, then log in and complete the Dietitian application.

steps.title: Submit Transcripts and Supervised Practice Verification
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts confirming your degree in dietetics or nutrition to be sent directly to the Board. Upload or mail documentation of your supervised practice experience (e.g., Verification Statement from a dietetic internship).

steps.title: Provide CDR Examination Verification
steps.description: Ensure the Missouri Board of Healing Arts receives verification of your successful completion of the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) examination. This is often done by providing a copy of your CDR registration card within the MoRLD system.

steps.title: Complete a Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the MoRLD system to complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check through an approved vendor. This typically involves registering with a state-approved service and having your fingerprints taken.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $30.00 via the MoRLD online payment system or by mail, as specified in your application.

steps.title: Board Review and License Issuance
steps.description: The Missouri Board of Healing Arts will review your complete application and all supporting documents. Upon approval, your Dietitian license will be issued and mailed to your address on file.

documents.detail: Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration (RD or RDN) credential.
Sent from the degree-granting institution showing completion of an ACEND-accredited program.
Documentation of completed ACEND-accredited dietetic internship hours.
Completed authorization for the state background screening process.

provisional_text: Missouri does not currently offer a provisional or temporary dietitian license. Applicants must hold active CDR registration before applying for state licensure.
reciprocity_text: Missouri does not have a formal reciprocity agreement with other states. Out-of-state dietitians must apply for a new Missouri license through the standard application process. Holding current CDR registration (RD/RDN) satisfies the core requirements. Note: Missouri is actively pursuing the Dietitian Licensure Compact (Senate Bill 412/1339), which would enable interstate practice without additional licenses if enacted.

faqs.question: How much does a Missouri dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application fee is $50. The biennial renewal fee is only $20, due by April 1 of even-numbered years. A $50 late fee applies if renewal is submitted after the 29-day grace period.

faqs.question: Does Missouri require continuing education for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No. The State Committee of Dietitians does not mandate continuing education for state license renewal. However, you must maintain your national CDR registration, which requires 75 CPE units every five years.

faqs.question: What is the minimum age to apply for a Missouri dietitian license?
faqs.answer: Missouri requires applicants to be at least 21 years old to be eligible for a Licensed Dietitian credential.

faqs.question: Does Missouri have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Missouri. To work in Missouri, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Missouri?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Missouri while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Missouri does not have a formal reciprocity agreement with other states. Out-of-state dietitians must apply for a new Missouri license through the standard application process. Holding current CDR registration (RD/RDN) satisfies the core requirements. Note: Missouri is actively pursuing the Dietitian Licensure Compact (Senate Bill 412/1339), which would enable interstate practice without additional licenses if enacted.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Missouri
hero.dek: To apply for an initial Dietitian license in Missouri, prospective licensees must apply online through the state's MoRLD (Missouri Real-time Licensing Database) system, managed by the Missouri Board of Healing Arts.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Missouri Board of Healing Arts initial application page and click the 'Apply Online' link to access the MoRLD system. If you are a new user, click 'New User Registration' to create an account. Follow the prompts to set up your profile, then log in to select and complete the Dietitian license application.
